req.title: Hr-Admin creates expense-claim
req.statement: An authenticated hr-admin shall be able to create a expense-claim through the UI form. Mandatory fields must be validated and an audit record must be written on success.

doc.reasoning: The requirement mandates that an authenticated hr-admin can create an expense-claim via the UI form, with mandatory field validation, audit record creation, and correct HTTP response codes. I derived: (1) a positive EP test covering a fully valid submission by an hr-admin; (2) a negative NEG test covering submission with all mandatory fields blank, expecting 400-level validation feedback; (3) a BVA boundary test probing the maximum-length description field (at-limit vs over-limit); (4) a DT decision-table test covering the 401/403 access-control matrix (unauthenticated and wrong-role actors); (5) an STC state-transition test verifying the workflow state moves from 'draft' through 'submitted' and that the audit record is written on that transition. Each test is self-contained via fixtures and helper placeholders.

tests.name: test_hr_admin_creates_valid_expense_claim
actions: Navigate to /expense-claims/new
Fill all mandatory fields with valid data
Submit the form
Capture the response status and returned entity id
Query the audit log for the new entity id
expected: Response body contains a non-empty entity id
Audit log contains one record referencing the entity id with action=CREATE
trace: R-WEB-041: response 200 or 201 with entity id
tests.executable:
import pytest
from playwright.sync_api import expect

def test_hr_admin_creates_valid_expense_claim(hr_admin_page, api_client, valid_expense_payload):
    page = hr_admin_page
    page.goto("/expense-claims/new")

    <fill_expense_form>(page, valid_expense_payload)

    with page.expect_response(
        lambda r: "/expense-claims" in r.url and r.request.method in ("POST", "PUT")
    ) as resp_info:
        page.get_by_role("button", name="Submit").click()

    response = resp_info.value
    assert response.status in (200, 201), f"Unexpected status: {response.status}"

    body = response.json()
    entity_id = body.get("id") or body.get("claimId")
    assert entity_id, "Response must contain an entity id"

    audit = api_client.get(f"/audit-log?entity={entity_id}&action=CREATE")
    assert audit.status_code == 200
    records = audit.json().get("records", [])
    assert len(records) >= 1, "Audit record must be written on successful creation"

tests.name: test_expense_claim_rejected_when_mandatory_fields_blank
actions: Navigate to /expense-claims/new
Leave all mandatory fields empty
Click Submit
Observe validation messages and response code
expected: Form does not navigate away (no successful submission)
Inline validation errors are displayed for each mandatory field
trace: R-WEB-041: Mandatory fields must be validated
tests.executable:
import pytest
from playwright.sync_api import expect

def test_expense_claim_rejected_when_mandatory_fields_blank(hr_admin_page, mandatory_field_selectors):
    page = hr_admin_page
    page.goto("/expense-claims/new")

    api_statuses = []
    page.on(
        "response",
        lambda r: api_statuses.append(r.status)
        if "/expense-claims" in r.url and r.request.method == "POST"
        else None,
    )

    page.get_by_role("button", name="Submit").click()
    page.wait_for_timeout(500)

    current_url = page.url
    assert "/expense-claims/new" in current_url, "Page must not navigate on invalid submit"

    for selector in mandatory_field_selectors:
        error_locator = page.locator(f"[data-error-for='{selector}']").or_(
            page.locator(f"#{selector}-error")
        )
        expect(error_locator).to_be_visible()

    for status in api_statuses:
        assert status == 400, f"Expected 400 but got {status}"

tests.name: test_expense_claim_description_at_max_length_boundary
preconditions: Maximum allowed description length is known (e.g. 500 characters)
actions: Navigate to /expense-claims/new
Fill mandatory fields with valid data
Set description to exactly MAX_LENGTH characters
Submit and verify success
Repeat with MAX_LENGTH + 1 characters and verify rejection
expected: Submission with exactly MAX_LENGTH characters returns 200 or 201
Submission with MAX_LENGTH+1 characters returns 400 or shows validation error
trace: R-WEB-041: Mandatory fields must be validated
tests.executable:
import pytest

MAX_LEN = 500

def test_expense_claim_description_at_max_length_boundary(
    hr_admin_page, api_client, valid_expense_payload
):
    page = hr_admin_page

    # --- At boundary (MAX_LEN): expect success ---
    payload_at = {**valid_expense_payload, "description": "x" * MAX_LEN}
    page.goto("/expense-claims/new")
    <fill_expense_form>(page, payload_at)

    with page.expect_response(
        lambda r: "/expense-claims" in r.url and r.request.method == "POST"
    ) as at_resp:
        page.get_by_role("button", name="Submit").click()

    assert at_resp.value.status in (200, 201), (
        f"Expected 200/201 at max length, got {at_resp.value.status}"
    )

    # --- Over boundary (MAX_LEN + 1): expect rejection ---
    payload_over = {**valid_expense_payload, "description": "x" * (MAX_LEN + 1)}
    page.goto("/expense-claims/new")
    <fill_expense_form>(page, payload_over)

    statuses = []
    page.on(
        "response",
        lambda r: statuses.append(r.status)
        if "/expense-claims" in r.url and r.request.method == "POST"
        else None,
    )
    page.get_by_role("button", name="Submit").click()
    page.wait_for_timeout(600)

    over_rejected = any(s == 400 for s in statuses) or "/expense-claims/new" in page.url
    assert over_rejected, "Submission over max length must be rejected"

tests.name: test_expense_claim_access_control_matrix
preconditions: An unauthenticated browser session is available
A browser session authenticated as a role WITHOUT hr-admin is available
actions: Attempt POST /expense-claims as unauthenticated user; expect 401
Attempt POST /expense-claims as non-hr-admin role; expect 403
expected: Request from forbidden role returns HTTP 403

tests.name: test_expense_claim_state_transition_draft_to_submitted
preconditions: A saved draft expense-claim exists for the hr-admin (state=draft)
actions: Navigate to the draft expense-claim detail page
Click 'Submit Claim' to transition state from draft to submitted
Capture response and reload the claim
Query the audit log for the state-change event
expected: Audit log records a SUBMIT action with old_state=draft and new_state=submitted
trace: R-WEB-041: response 200 or 201 with entity id
tests.executable:
import pytest
from playwright.sync_api import expect

def test_expense_claim_state_transition_draft_to_submitted(
    hr_admin_page, api_client, draft_expense_claim
):
    claim_id = draft_expense_claim["id"]
    page = hr_admin_page

    page.goto(f"/expense-claims/{claim_id}")
    state_badge = page.locator("[data-testid='claim-state']").or_(
        page.locator(".claim-status")
    )
    expect(state_badge).to_contain_text("draft", ignore_case=True)

    with page.expect_response(
        lambda r: f"/expense-claims/{claim_id}" in r.url
        and r.request.method in ("POST", "PATCH", "PUT")
    ) as resp_info:
        page.get_by_role("button", name="Submit Claim").click()

    response = resp_info.value
    assert response.status in (200, 201), f"Unexpected status: {response.status}"

    page.reload()
    expect(state_badge).to_contain_text("submitted", ignore_case=True)

    audit = api_client.get(
        f"/audit-log?entity={claim_id}&action=SUBMIT"
    )
    assert audit.status_code == 200
    records = audit.json().get("records", [])
    assert any(
        r.get("old_state") == "draft" and r.get("new_state") == "submitted"
        for r in records
    ), "Audit log must record draft->submitted transition"
